expectations.text: A regression test for the specific bug is written BEFORE any fix is applied
expectations.evidence: Transcript shows 'Creating initial bug reproduction test' (msg 10) before 'Fixing duration parsing math' (msg 13). Test file was created first with TestParseDuration_FractionalHours.

expectations.text: The regression test name clearly describes the bug (e.g., TestParseDuration_HandlesFractionalHours)
expectations.evidence: Test is named TestParseDuration_FractionalHours — clearly describes what's being tested.

expectations.text: The regression test is run and confirmed to FAIL before the fix
expectations.evidence: Transcript shows 'Noticing test run failure' (msg 11) after creating the test and before applying the fix.

expectations.text: The fix uses strconv.ParseFloat instead of strconv.Atoi (as the bug description suggests)
expectations.evidence: duration.go line 39: num, err := strconv.ParseFloat(numStr, 64) — correctly replaced strconv.Atoi.

expectations.text: The fix is minimal — only changes what's needed to handle fractional values
expectations.evidence: Fix changes: (1) strconv.Atoi → strconv.ParseFloat, (2) time.Duration(num) * time.Hour → time.Duration(num * float64(time.Hour)). Same pattern for m and s cases. No other changes.

expectations.text: Existing behaviors are preserved (integer durations like '2h' still work)
expectations.evidence: strconv.ParseFloat handles integer strings (e.g., '2') correctly, returning 2.0. The math is identical for whole numbers.

expectations.text: Additional edge cases are tested (e.g., '0.5m', '2.25h', fractional seconds)
expectations.evidence: Only one test: TestParseDuration_FractionalHours for '1.5h'. No tests for fractional minutes, fractional seconds, or other fractional hour values like '2.25h'.

expectations.text: All tests pass after the fix
expectations.evidence: Transcript shows 'Completing task and running tests' (msg 14) and 'Marking todo as complete' (msg 17), indicating tests passed.
